title: Context editing + memory tool to prevent context exhaustion in long agent runs
source: Anthropic context management (context editing / memory tool beta) — docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/context-editing; live fetch blocked this run
description: Long delegate/team runs accumulate large tool-result blocks (file reads, command output) that eventually exhaust the context window and force truncation, degrading output quality on L/XL tickets. Anthropic's context-editing feature auto-clears stale tool results once a token threshold is hit, and the memory tool lets an agent persist facts to files across the run. This directly improves reliability of the longest, highest-value tickets.
proposed_changes: In delegate_to_agent, enable context editing on L/XL/complexity-gated runs (clear tool-result blocks above a configurable token trigger, keeping the most recent N). Wire the memory tool to the existing .cto/session or a per-ticket .cto/scratchpad directory so a Morty can offload intermediate findings to a file rather than holding them in-context. Add a config flag in .cto/config.json (context_editing.enabled, trigger_tokens, keep_recent) and emit a roro event when context is compacted so the Rick Terminal can visualize it.
reject_reason: Apply failed: Evolve delegation failed: (no stderr)
